name: Lord Howe Standard Time
dstRules: Observes 30-minute DST shift (UTC+11 in summer, Oct–Apr) — the only place using a 30-minute DST change
description: Used exclusively by Lord Howe Island, a tiny Australian territory in the Tasman Sea with about 400 residents. Uniquely, Lord Howe Island advances clocks by only 30 minutes for DST (to UTC+11), rather than the standard one-hour shift.
